phase-scope.phase-33: Admin MTProto operations and observability are implemented behind admin-only APIs and compact frontend-admin screens with strict secret redaction.
phase-scope.phase-34: Stabilization gate keeps admin MTProto operations in the release-readiness suite and verifies they do not expose owner-only proxy secrets.
phase-scope.phase-40: Official dd-secret admin revoke/reissue sync was verified locally but is superseded for production by Phase-41.
phase-scope.phase-41: Admin operations show official-mode assignments as superseded/reissue-required and target KPprotoN SNI policy for revoke/reissue.
phase-scope.phase-43: Admin operations add alert review actions, confirmation-safe proxy disable, and TTL IP block guardrails backed by M-060/M-061.

commands.command-1: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py tests/test_admin_device_control.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py -v
commands.command-4: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-33.xml docs/plans/Phase-41.xml docs/modules/M-047.xml docs/verification/V-M-047.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml && mygrace lint

verification-levels.module-local: Backend tests prove admin-only MTProto list/detail/health/reissue/revoke behavior, audit writes, secret-free payloads, and failure mapping.
verification-levels.wave-level: M-042, M-043, M-044, and M-024 regression tests stay in the gate so admin operations preserve assignment persistence, provisioning reissue policy, runtime bridge health, and confirmation-safe admin mutation patterns.
verification-levels.phase-level: Frontend-admin build and smoke prove the MTProto operator page is compact on mobile, exposes only redacted data, and requires explicit confirmation for destructive actions.
verification-levels.phase-41-level: Phase-41 gates must prove admin reissue/revoke targets KPprotoN SNI policy, official dd state is marked superseded, and admin output remains credential-free.
verification-levels.phase-43-level: Phase-43 gates must prove alert actions are confirmation-safe, audited, scoped to one assignment/IP evidence item, and do not expose decrypted IPs in audit details.

module-checks.check-1: Only authenticated admins can access MTProto admin endpoints; normal users and unauthenticated requests are rejected.
module-checks.check-2: Assignment list and detail endpoints filter by user, SNI, status, and timestamps while returning only redacted or derived-safe fields.
module-checks.check-3: Runtime health endpoint exposes aggregate bridge status, adapter name, safe failure codes, and timestamps without proxy credentials.
module-checks.check-4: Admin reissue requires explicit confirmation and returns a redacted assignment state instead of a full owner proxy link or derived secret.
module-checks.check-5: Admin revoke disables only the selected MTProto assignment, removes its runtime SNI policy, and does not cancel KrotPN subscription, VPN devices, trial state, or the user account.
module-checks.check-6: Reissue and revoke create admin audit records whose details omit tg links, raw secrets, verification tokens, and session credentials.
module-checks.check-7: Backend log markers exist for list filtering, redaction, runtime health, explicit reissue, explicit revoke, and audited outcomes.
module-checks.check-8: Frontend-admin MTProto page renders a compact table/card hybrid with search, status filters, health summary, and confirmation-safe actions at mobile widths.
module-checks.check-9: Frontend API/types and UI never render, log, or persist full tg://proxy links, base secrets, derived secrets, or secret salts in admin surfaces.
module-checks.check-10: Phase-34 full gate verifies admin inspect/reissue/revoke remains consistent with owner dashboard, runtime bridge, and assignment registry state.
module-checks.check-11: Admin list/detail exposes safe credential mode and superseded official state without owner proxy links or secrets.
module-checks.check-12: Admin reissue converts exactly one official-mode or stale assignment to KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI policy and returns only redacted state.
module-checks.check-13: Admin revoke removes exactly one KPprotoN SNI policy through M-044 and does not call official manifest sync as the normal production path.
module-checks.check-14: Admin API, audit records, DOM snapshots, console traces, and smoke output contain no tg links, ee secrets, dd secrets, base secret, salt, runtime token, or private key material.
module-checks.check-15: Alert acknowledge and resolve actions update only M-060 review state and write safe audit records.
module-checks.check-16: Disable proxy from an alert delegates to the existing revoke path and affects exactly one MTProto assignment.
module-checks.check-17: TTL IP block refuses missing, stale, untrusted, or non-M-061 IP evidence and requires double confirmation.
module-checks.check-18: Audit details for IP-related actions contain observation ids/hash prefixes, not decrypted raw IP strings.

phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-1: Admin surfaces must not disclose owner-only tg://proxy links or derived fake-TLS secrets even when Phase-34 creates real owner assignments in test fixtures.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-2: Admin revoke must remain scoped to MTProto assignment/runtime policy state and must not cancel KrotPN trial, subscription, account, or VPN device access.

phase-41-kpproton-gates.gate-1: Official credential-mode assignments are visible to admins only as superseded/reissue-required safe state.
phase-41-kpproton-gates.gate-2: Reissue writes a KPprotoN fake-TLS assignment state for exactly one user and keeps owner credentials out of admin responses.
phase-41-kpproton-gates.gate-3: Revoke removes one SNI policy via the KPprotoN runtime bridge and leaves VPN account/subscription state untouched.
phase-41-kpproton-gates.gate-4: Frontend-admin /mtproto remains compact on mobile and keeps destructive actions confirmation-safe.

scenarios.scenario-1: Admin opens the MTProto page, sees runtime bridge health and assignment counters, and searches by email, user id, SNI fragment, or status.
scenarios.scenario-2: Admin views one assignment detail with assignment id, user context, SNI, status, rotation marker, timestamps, and bridge-safe health only.
scenarios.scenario-3: Admin reissues one assignment after explicit confirmation and the response marks the new safe assignment state without exposing owner-only credentials.
scenarios.scenario-4: Admin revokes one MTProto assignment after explicit confirmation; the runtime SNI policy is removed while the user's VPN subscription and devices remain active.
scenarios.scenario-5: Non-admin, unauthenticated, missing-confirmation, unknown-assignment, and conflicting-SNI paths return stable safe errors.
scenarios.scenario-6: Runtime bridge degraded or unavailable state is visible as a safe admin warning and does not block list/detail reads or leak runtime internals.
scenarios.scenario-7: Secret rotation stale state prompts reissue-required handling and never shows stale tg links in admin output.
scenarios.scenario-8: Phase-41 admin reissue converts one superseded official assignment to KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI policy without returning owner credentials.
scenarios.scenario-9: Phase-43 admin acknowledges and resolves an abuse alert without changing runtime access.
scenarios.scenario-10: Phase-43 admin disables a proxy from a reviewed alert with confirmation and safe audit evidence.
scenarios.scenario-11: Phase-43 IP block fails closed when IP evidence is unavailable, stale, untrusted, or missing TTL confirmation.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Admin API responses may include assignment id, user id, redacted or explicit user email per existing admin policy, SNI, status, credential mode, rotation marker, timestamps, safe bridge status, and safe failure codes.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Admin API responses, audit records, backend logs, frontend console logs, smoke traces, and analytics labels must not include full tg://proxy links, https://t.me/proxy links, ee or dd secrets, MTPROTO_BASE_SECRET_HEX, MTPROTO_SECRET_SALT, runtime tokens, email verification tokens, JWT/session tokens, or certificate private keys.
redaction-rules.rule-3: Owner-only MTProto secret and link payloads remain limited to the authenticated user cabinet contract in M-045; M-047 must not create an admin backdoor for reading those credentials.
redaction-rules.rule-4: Reissue/revoke audit details must use safe identifiers and action outcomes, not raw request bodies that could contain future secret-bearing fields.
redaction-rules.rule-5: Phase-43 alert/IP action audit details must not include decrypted raw IP strings; use M-061 observation ids and hash/prefix evidence only.

ui-smoke-gates.gate-1: MTProto admin page at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932 has document scrollWidth <= innerWidth.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-2: Search, status filter, runtime health, assignment row actions, and confirmation modal remain reachable within the compact admin shell.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-3: Reissue and revoke require an explicit confirmation surface with the selected assignment/user context visible before mutation.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-4: Mobile layout avoids desktop-only wide tables, nested cards, hero blocks, and oversized explanatory text.
ui-smoke-gates.gate-5: Secret-shaped values and tg://proxy strings are absent from rendered admin DOM snapshots and smoke output.

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: admin_mtproto_list_count tagged by status/filter shape without user secrets.
telemetry-requirements.metric-2: admin_mtproto_health_count tagged by bridge status and safe failure code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-3: admin_mtproto_reissue_count tagged by result and safe failure code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-4: admin_mtproto_revoke_count tagged by result and safe failure code.
telemetry-requirements.metric-5: admin_mtproto_ui_action_count tagged by action and confirmation outcome without secret-bearing labels.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe-local: Backend tests with local DB fixtures, frontend-admin build, XML parsing, MyGRACE lint, and the phase smoke script are safe to run locally.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Live production reissue/revoke against real users, public edge runtime changes, DNS/TLS edits, deploy scripts, install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, and Telegram Desktop/mobile proof remain out of scope unless the operator explicitly approves them.

phase-33-acceptance-gates.gate-1: All commands in this verification file pass before Phase-33 can move from planned to pass-local.
phase-33-acceptance-gates.gate-2: No admin payload, log, audit row, frontend DOM snapshot, or smoke artifact contains tg://proxy, fake-TLS secrets, base secret, or salt.
phase-33-acceptance-gates.gate-3: Revoke and reissue are audited, confirmation-safe, and scoped to MTProto assignment plus MTProto runtime policy state only.
phase-33-acceptance-gates.gate-4: Frontend-admin navigation includes a compact MTProto surface without regressing existing dashboard, users, devices, analytics, servers, or plans routes.

evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-14: V-M-047 backend gate passed locally with 16 tests across MTProto admin ops, admin device control, assignment registry, and runtime bridge, including runtime SNI policy removal on revoke.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-14: frontend-admin npm run build passed and generated production assets without tracked dist changes.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-14: scripts/phase33-mtproto-admin-smoke.mjs passed, including protected deploy/install/edge surface guard.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-14: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and git diff whitespace checks passed.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-05-14: Phase-34 static smoke passed locally and confirmed admin MTProto UI/API surfaces do not expose owner-only tg links or derived secrets.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design adds admin gates for official-mode supersession, KPprotoN reissue/revoke policy behavior, redaction, and mobile compactness.
evidence.evidence-8: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local admin tests passed for KPprotoN bridge health, reissue/revoke through M-044, audit-safe redaction, and no official manifest sync in the normal production path.
